text.title: MCP Server Catalog Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team copies a curated-list blog post; some servers are 18 months stale, two flag false-positive on gitleaks.
antipattern.fix: Re-score every entry: security_score 0-3 + last_release + recommendation. Refresh quarterly.

antipattern.symptom: Catalog lists a server exposing 60+ tools across 6 unrelated domains. Team installs it; tool-selection collapses.
antipattern.root-cause: No tool-count cap on catalog entries.
antipattern.fix: Flag servers with > 30 tools as `monitor`; prefer focused alternatives in the same category.

antipattern.symptom: Catalog entry hides that the server requires `admin:*` scope. Team installs; a misfire deletes prod data.
antipattern.fix: Require `env_vars` list + scope-min metadata. Avoid recommendation if write-all required and read-only alt exists.

antipattern.symptom: Catalog hasn't been touched in 8 months. 4 servers archived upstream; team installs broken.
antipattern.fix: Named reviewer + quarterly refresh date in CI; stale catalogs auto-flag.
